dev_plans.content: Lightweight heuristic approach — single-scalar AUROC gap with a hard per-group support gate. Measures the worst-case disparity between the overall test AUROC and the most disadvantaged minority group (black or hispanic). Computed per epoch on the validation loader, recorded via a probe module that mirrors the existing `prober.py` interface (record/conclude). Pipeline insertion: call `record(epoch, model, val_loader, device)` immediately after `evaluate()` in train.py's epoch loop. Implementation: (1) run a no-grad forward pass over the unshuffled val loader to collect `y_score`, `y_true`, and `group_idx` (argmax of `loader.dataset.eth`). (2) For each of 5 groups, compute AUROC only if n_pos>=20 AND n_neg>=20, else mark None (matches MIN_SAMPLES gate in prober.py). (3) gap = overall_auroc - min(black_auroc, hispanic_auroc); NaN if both minority groups lack support. (4) Append (epoch, gap) to `_SERIES`, rewrite `live/probe_live.json` atomically, append row to `logs/fairness_subgroup.csv`. (5) At end-of-training `conclude(0.03, 0.07)` writes `metric/probe_result_N.json` with status=PASS if tail_mean(gap over last 5 epochs) <= 0.03, plus a Plotly PDF chart in `plot/` with two dashed threshold lines. Rationale: matches the existing MASK_MINORITY_TFIDF path directly — if masking hurts minority AUROC, the gap widens; if the linear layer's per-group bias compensates, gap stays tight. No reweighting, no bootstrap — just a single readable scalar.
dev_plans.metric: subgroup_auroc_gap = overall_val_AUROC - min(black_val_AUROC, hispanic_val_AUROC), computed per epoch on the unshuffled validation loader (lower is better)

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous approach — equal-opportunity RECALL gap with bootstrap CI and an AUROC-maintenance guard. Per the project's established finding, per-group AUROC on MIMIC is inherently near-fair (gap ~0.04 regardless of intervention), so AUROC alone is insensitive; recall at the model's operating threshold is the honest bias lever and is also gameable by leveling-down (collapsing AUROC), so the metric is paired with a guard. Metric: `equal_opportunity_recall_gap = recall_white - recall_pooled_minority` (black+hispanic pooled to lift the tiny hispanic stratum above the MIN_SAMPLES floor), evaluated at a shared operating threshold fixed across epochs at ~20% predicted-positive rate (calibrated on epoch-1 val scores, then frozen). Pipeline insertion: per-epoch `record()` over val_loader, BUT conclude() runs once on the best checkpoint (loaded via `model.load_state_dict(torch.load(ckpt_path))` after training) with 1000-iteration stratified bootstrap over the val set to get a 95% CI on the gap. Implementation: (1) collect y_score, y_true, group_idx as in plan 1. (2) threshold = the score quantile that yields 20% predicted-positive rate on epoch-1 val; cache it. (3) per epoch compute recall_white and recall_minority at threshold; gap = recall_white - recall_minority; append to series. (4) conclude: resample val indices with stratification by (group, label) 1000x, recompute gap, report `gap_p50` and `gap_p95_upper`. (5) GUARD: also compute overall_val_AUROC on best ckpt; if overall_AUROC < 0.78 (degradation > 0.04 vs the ~0.82-0.84 baseline), force status=FAIL even if gap passes — this catches the glm-style leveling-down failure mode. Artifacts: `probe_result_N.json` with {gap, gap_ci_upper, overall_auroc, guard_failed, status}, Plotly PDF with gap series + CI band, per-group AUROC CSV at `ethnic_auroc/<run>_<round>_AUROC.csv` (one row per group) to match the existing collect_auroc.py artifact contract.
dev_plans.metric: equal_opportunity_recall_gap = recall_white - recall_pooled_minority (black+hispanic), at a fixed 20%-predicted-positive-rate threshold shared across epochs, computed per epoch on the validation loader (lower is better)

dev_plans.content: Tooling/library-based approach — Fairlearn's `MetricFrame` + `demographic_parity` / `equalized_odds` differences as the per-epoch scalar, with `SelectionRate` and `true_positive_rate` as the underlying frame metrics. Uses `fairlearn.metrics.MetricFrame` to bucket val predictions by the ethnicity one-hot and compute `equalized_odds_difference()` (max TPR/FPR disparity across groups) as the headline scalar. Pipeline insertion: per-epoch `record(epoch, model, val_loader, device)` after `evaluate()`. Implementation: (1) `pip install fairlearn scikit-learn` (fairlearn is the de-facto library — wraps sklearn metrics with group-aware bookkeeping). (2) Forward pass over unshuffled val loader → y_true, y_score, group_idx; derive y_pred = (y_score >= 0.5). (3) Build a `MetricFrame(metrics={'auroc': roc_auc_score, 'recall': recall_score, 'selection_rate': selection_rate}, y_true=y_true, y_pred=y_pred, sensitive_features=group_idx)`. (4) Headline scalar = `equalized_odds_difference(y_true, y_pred, sensitive_features=group_idx)` — the max disparity in TPR and FPR across the 5 groups; this is Fairlearn's recommended single-number fairness summary. (5) Secondary scalar = `MetricFrame.difference(method='between_groups')['auroc']` (max-min AUROC across groups) — stored in CSV for the AUROC-maintenance guard. (6) Append (epoch, equalized_odds_diff, auroc_diff) to `_SERIES`; live JSON + CSV mirror the existing prober contract. (7) `conclude(0.10, 0.18)`: status=PASS if tail_mean(equalized_odds_diff) <= 0.10 AND tail_mean(auroc_diff) <= 0.06 (guard); writes probe_result_N.json and a Plotly PDF. Artifacts also include a `MetricFrame.by_group` table dumped to CSV (per-group auroc/recall/selection_rate/support per epoch) — directly auditable and matching the per-group CSVs already collected by `ethnic_auroc/collect_auroc.py`.
dev_plans.metric: equalized_odds_difference = max(TPR_dispersion, FPR_dispersion) across the 5 ethnicity groups on the validation loader, computed per epoch via fairlearn.metrics.equalized_odds_difference (lower is better)
